title: Adopting-beneficiary outreach + partner shortlist (training programs, OER, advocacy)
context: The Elyos definition of shipped requires that explainers be adopted and used by a named beneficiary — an explainer library that no one teaches from is not a delivered good deed. Currently no named training program, education committee, journal-club network, OER repository, or advocacy organization has agreed to adopt the explainers (verifiedNeed: false portfolio-wide). M0 includes explicit outreach to de-risk the cold-start scenario. Even if a formal partner cannot be secured in M0, a published CC-BY explainer pair with checklist cards creates a self-serve fallback that an educator can adopt directly. This task produces a partner shortlist and opens at least one documented outreach thread.
objective: Identify and document a prioritized shortlist of candidate adopting beneficiaries (training programs, education committees, journal-club networks, OER repositories, and advocacy organizations), and open at least one documented outreach thread with a candidate — providing the evidence trail that supports transitioning tasks to verifiedNeed: true once a partner confirms adoption.
acceptanceCriteria: A committed shortlist document names >= 3 candidate adopting beneficiary types with specific example organizations or programs in each category: oncology residency/fellowship training programs and their education/critical-appraisal faculty; education committees of professional societies; open journal-club / evidence-based-medicine communities; OER medical-education repositories (MedEdPORTAL-style); patient-advocacy organizations that train advocates to read the science.

For each shortlisted candidate, the document records: name, type, contact approach, rationale for fit with the explainer set, and the adoption mechanism (curriculum embed / journal-club kit / OER repository acceptance).
At least one outreach thread is opened and documented: the communication channel, the contact, the date, the explainer set description shared, and the requested adoption commitment.
If a candidate declines or does not respond, that is documented as evidence (not suppressed); the self-serve fallback (published CC-BY explainer pair) is noted as the alternative path.
The outreach document and shortlist are committed to the project repo; the maintainer/steward has reviewed the outreach approach.
The task is prerequisite to partner-013 (secure first confirmed adopting beneficiary).
resources: PLAN.md — Problem & beneficiaries, Partner/requestor, Roadmap M0 cold-start
TASKS.md — outreach-008 row and verifiedNeed discussion
Students 4 Best Evidence-style networks; EQUATOR/reporting-guideline community contacts
OER medical education repositories; professional society education committees
output: A committed partner-shortlist and outreach-log document, with >= 3 candidate adopting beneficiaries shortlisted and >= 1 outreach thread opened and documented. This is the prerequisite evidence trail for partner-013 (securing the first confirmed beneficiary).
